design_philosophy.core_loop: Swim, sing/echolocate to navigate and interact with environment, ram enemies, manage air meter, solve glyph-based puzzles to progress through ocean levels toward alien confrontation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Dual resource management: air meter (drowning risk) vs health meter (enemy damage). Exploration-based discovery of solutions paired with unpredictable enemy encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody an intelligent, capable dolphin using echolocation and song to understand and survive an alien-invaded ocean ecosystem, progressing from local mystery to cosmic conflict
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Refuses difficulty assistance or tutorials; punishment-heavy design with instant-kill scenarios; forces player discovery of game mechanics through play. Couples realistic marine biology (air-breathing, cetacean communication) with sci-fi narrative. Environmental storytelling through glyph libraries and NPC dialogue drives plot exposition rather than cutscenes

difficulty.lives_system: Health meter depleted by enemy contact or air deprivation; continues available but with level restart
difficulty.continues_system: Player can continue from level checkpoints after losing all health
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing; no mid-level saves documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No difficulty settings; progression locked behind mandatory boss encounters and glyph-puzzle solutions. Song upgrades provide optional power scaling (ranged attack, enemy freeze/stun) but are not mandatory

progression.upgrade_system: Two optional song upgrades available at specific points: (1) charged song as long-range weapon, (2) song to temporarily stun/freeze enemies. Health regeneration and underwater breathing unlocked after Asterite encounter
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated progression through glyph keys that must be located to pass environmental barriers; narrative keys (meeting Big Blue, finding Asterite, time travel activation) unlock new ocean regions and abilities
replayability_hooks: Multiple glyph locations to discover for stat boosts and invulnerability
Optional song upgrades that alter combat strategy
Hidden air vents and health sources reward thorough exploration
Time travel mechanic creates past/present level variants

world_building_techniques: NPC dialogue with other marine life (dolphins, orca, Big Blue whale) establishes world rules and quest objectives
Environmental storytelling: glyph library in Atlantis contains alien history and exposition
Cutscenes frame major narrative beats (waterspout abduction, final Vortex Queen encounter)
Creature interaction through song mechanic ties gameplay to narrative (communicating with pteranodon in past, receiving blessing from Asterite)
Mythology/legend integration: Big Blue as wise elder, Asterite as primordial life form, Atlantis as lost civilization anchor

boss_design.philosophy: Bosses enforce puzzle-solving and pattern recognition rather than pure reflexes. Asterite and Vortex Queen require understanding their attack patterns and exploiting environmental mechanics or song-upgrades. Enemy variety (seahorses, octopods, sharks) scaled through difficulty zones
notable_bosses: Asterite (ancient life form, requires ramming attack to dislodge orbs; shifts to ally role mid-game)
Vortex Queen (final boss, alien monarch encountered on Vortex planet)

faction_indicators: Marine ecosystem pod (dolphins, whales, cetaceans) = ally faction
danger_signals: Waterspout abduction (environmental catastrophe signaling external threat)
Aggressive marine life (sharks, octopods) as proxy enemies before Vortex reveal
Ocean chaos spreading across regions signals escalating crisis
environmental_storytelling: Sunken Atlantis ruins establish fallen civilization narrative
Glyph crystals scattered across levels serve as lore repositories and puzzle objects
Transition from familiar ocean biomes (bay, arctic) to alien spacecraft signals narrative escalation
Time travel sequences (prehistoric era) contextualize ancient threat cycle within deep history

sources_used: wikipedia: Ecco the Dolphin gameplay section (mechanics, air system, song upgrades, glyph system)
wikipedia: Ecco the Dolphin plot section (narrative structure, boss encounters, world-building)
